title: Houston Rockets vs. Golden State Warriors Live Score, Stats & Gametracker for April 5, 2026
body:
In a thrilling matchup that will be etched in the memories of NBA fans for a long time, the Houston Rockets pulled off a stunning upset against their Western Conference rivals, the Golden State Warriors. The 107-105 victory, sealed with a go-ahead layup by Alperen Sengun with just 11 seconds remaining, marked a remarkable turnaround in fortunes for the Rockets, who have been struggling to maintain consistency throughout the season.

The turning point came in the dying minutes of the game when Kevin Durant, the former NBA superstar now plying his trade with the Rockets, found Sengun with a pinpoint pass that allowed the talented big man to drive past the Warriors' defense and score the decisive basket. This dramatic twist not only handed the Rockets their first win in four games but also spoiled the highly anticipated return of Golden State's Stephen Curry, who had been sidelined for more than a week with an injury.

Curry's return was highly anticipated, and his fans were eager to see him in action once again. However, despite his best efforts, Curry couldn't quite get the Warriors over the line, and his team's inability to capitalize on their opponents' mistakes ultimately proved costly. The Rockets, on the other hand, capitalized on their chances and showed a resilience that has been lacking in their recent performances. Their victory is a testament to their ability to adapt and respond to pressure situations, qualities that will be essential in the remainder of the season.

The Rockets' win has significant implications for the Western Conference standings, where the Warriors were hoping to maintain their grip on a top spot. With this defeat, Golden State will need to regroup and reassess their strategy to stay ahead of their rivals. Meanwhile, the Rockets have breathed new life into their playoff hopes, and their young core, led by Sengun and Jamal Alvarado, will be eager to build on this momentum.

The return of Curry marked a significant storyline in this game, and his fans were eagerly awaiting his first appearance in over a week. Despite his best efforts, however, Curry was unable to lead his team to victory, and his shooting percentage suffered as a result of the Rockets' stifling defense. The Warriors' offense, which has been one of the most potent in the league this season, was unable to get into a rhythm, and they were forced to rely on their bench to try and mount a comeback.

As the NBA season enters its final stretch, the Rockets' victory over the Warriors will be a significant confidence booster for the team. With their young core showing signs of improvement and their veteran players stepping up when it matters most, the Rockets are well-positioned to make a run in the playoffs. Whether they can sustain this momentum and challenge for a spot in the Western Conference finals remains to be seen, but one thing is certain – their victory over the Warriors has given them a newfound sense of purpose and determination.
